commit.message:
Refactor for readability, add diagrams and white paper

Major refactoring following programming principles (readability first,
small functions, DRY, meaningful names, no scripts):

Code changes:
- design.py: Break 220-line recurrent_mlp() into 10 focused functions
  (_validate_config, _define_kernels, _create_direct_fifos,
   _create_memtile_fifos, _make_worker_body, _column_activation_tap,
   _weight_tap, _create_runtime_sequence, etc.)
- test.py: Replace hardcoded paths with IRON_DIR env var + Path discovery;
  extract generate_test_data(), run_npu(), run_cpu(), check_correctness(),
  compute_metrics(), print_results() from monolithic benchmark()
- op.py: Add class/method docstrings, extract _artifact_name(), use class
  constants for compiler flags
- __init__.py: Improve module docstring, add assertion messages, rename
  block_r/block_c to block_rows/block_cols
- mlp_kernels.cc: Document SIMD width, alignment requirements, kernel
  contracts; rename size/count to num_elements

Documentation:
- docs/generate_figures.py: Creates 3 high-quality matplotlib diagrams
  (hardware architecture, recurrent MLP dataflow, performance scaling)
- docs/generate_whitepaper.py: Generates PDF white paper via weasyprint
  combining architecture explanation, diagrams, and benchmark results
- README.md: Replace ASCII art with diagram images, add project structure
  section, link to white paper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES support for Mock Cluster (#2099)

### Issue

#2070 

### Description

Add support for TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES when creating mock and simulation
clusters as well. So far, target_devices were taking just the chips
specified in that set, not the ones from the boards where target chips
are. After this PR, user can use both TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES and
target_device_ids for creating mock and simulation cluster. When all
users transition to TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES, we could remove the support for
that.

### List of the changes

- Support TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES for mock and simulation cluster
- Refactor logic around creating cluster descriptor
- Take the chips from the same board where target devices are

commit.message:
fix: use statusRef in search debounce to avoid stale closure

The search debounce captured status from its mount-time closure,
so if the user changed the status filter within 300ms of page load
the debounce would fire with the stale 'all' value, overwriting the
correct filtered results. Fix by mirroring the existing searchRef
pattern for status.

commit.message:
chore: migrate from @variantjs/vue to @nuxt/ui (#2919)

### 🔗 Linked issue

### 📚 Description

Removes `@variantjs/vue` and `@variantjs/core` entirely, replacing all
usages with equivalent Nuxt UI components.

**Component replacements:**
- `t-alert` → `UAlert`
- `t-checkbox` → `UCheckbox`
- `t-dropdown` → `UDropdownMenu`
- `t-input` → `UInput`
- `t-input-group` → `UFormField`
- `t-select` → `USelect`
- `t-tag` → `UBadge`
- `t-textarea` → `UTextarea`

**Files changed:**
- `plugins/tailwind.ts` — removed all variantjs config/registrations;
retains only the custom `TTable.vue` registration
- `components/*.stories.vue` — story names preserved; components under
test swapped to Nuxt UI equivalents
- `components/NavBar.vue`, `DocumentEditor.vue`,
`DocumentEditorInput.vue`, `DocumentView.vue`, `PasswordInput.vue` —
inline usages replaced
- `pages/user/register.vue`, `forgot-password.vue`,
`change-password/[token].vue` — form field/alert patterns migrated
- `app.config.ts` — updated Nuxt UI color mappings from custom semantic
names (`'error'`, `'success'`, etc.) to actual Tailwind palette names
(`'red'`, `'green'`, etc.) to restore full color shade generation

> `t-nuxtlink` (custom `defineNuxtLink`) and `t-table` (custom
`TTable.vue`) were not variantjs components and are unchanged.

**Color fix:** The old `plugins/tailwind.ts` contained CSS class strings
(e.g. `bg-error-50`, `text-error-700`) as part of the variantjs
configuration, which caused Tailwind v4 to generate all color utility
classes and their corresponding CSS variables. Removing this file caused
only shades 300 and 600 to be generated for semantic colors, breaking
Nuxt UI component colors. The fix maps semantic colors to actual
Tailwind palette names (`error → red`, `success → green`, `warning →
amber`, `secondary → rose`, `info → sky`) so that `--ui-color-*`
variables always resolve to the fully-included standard Tailwind
palette.

commit.message:
Clean up rewards after PBRS implementation (#163)

* Tune rewards to encourage room exploration

Three changes to fix the model stalling at room 5 (0x38):

1. Reduce wrong-location penalty: -1.00 → -0.50
   The -1.00 cliff made exploring unknown exits net-negative (50% chance
   of -1.00 vs 50% chance of +0.75). At -0.50, trying new exits has
   positive expected value (+0.125), encouraging exploration.

2. Increase new-location reward: +0.75 → +1.00
   One successful room discovery should decisively outweigh penalties
   accumulated during exploration attempts.

3. Remove sword miss penalty entirely (-0.06/miss)
   Misses already cost time (animation lock + tile revisit penalties).
   The flat miss penalty made the model risk-averse about swinging,
   which hurts combat learning especially early in training. Removes
   torch dependency from critics.py.

* Sort model files by step count descending in debugger browser

Models with no step count appear first, followed by highest to lowest
step count. Previously used alphabetical order which didn't match
numerical checkpoint ordering.

* Fix diagnose.py to use pbrs_wavefront for PBRS diagnostic

The --infinite-pbrs mode was using prev.wavefront (includes items) instead
of prev.pbrs_wavefront (item-free, matches critics.py). This caused false
non-stationarity warnings from dropped items. Also removes unused
ObjectiveKind import.

* Enable key usage reward for dungeon progression

Uncomment critique_used_key (+0.25 for using a key at a locked door).
The model in dungeon1-skip-opening reaches the locked north door in room
0x63 at distance 4 but has no signal to walk through it despite having
3 keys. This provides the missing incentive.

* Lower tile revisit threshold from 8 to 4

Threshold 8 was tuned for the old system which had a -0.01/step
asymmetry on every move. Without that baseline cost, large rooms
(50+ tiles) let the model distribute visits broadly enough to stay
under threshold indefinitely. At threshold 4, penalties start around
step ~200 and escalate, making prolonged wandering expensive without
punishing short-term backtracking.

This is a separate commit for easy rollback if too aggressive.

* Remove dynamic LR adjustment, use fixed learning rate

The _adjust_learning_rate method had a bug where it read room-result
(an EnumMetric) instead of success-rate, causing LR to drop to minimum
at 0% actual success. Rather than fix the heuristic, remove it entirely
in favor of a fixed LR (0.0001). LR scheduling can be revisited later
with a proper approach (linear decay, cosine, etc.).

Removed: _adjust_learning_rate(), LEARNING_RATE_LOW, LEARNING_RATE_MINIMUM,
dynamic_lr kwarg, --high-lr CLI flag.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add small sword miss penalty (-0.01) when enemies present

Swinging the sword and missing when enemies are in the room now incurs
a REWARD_MINIMUM (-0.01) penalty. This gives slight negative pressure
to discourage flailing without meaningfully punishing exploration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove room 0x73 restriction from dungeon1-skip-opening LeftPlayArea

Only end the scenario when the agent actually leaves dungeon 1 (level != 1),
not when it enters room 0x73 (one tile south of start).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove ATTACK_NO_ENEMIES_PENALTY (sword masked when no enemies)

Sword actions are already masked off when no enemies are present,
making this penalty dead code. The miss penalty (-0.01) remains
for swings that miss when enemies are nearby.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace tile revisit penalty with room step stalling penalty

The per-tile revisit penalty was exploitable by looping through many
distinct tiles. Replace with a room-level step counter: after 150
steps in the same room, penalty escalates as -0.01 * excess/50.
Counter resets on room change, enemy kills (fewer active enemies),
or item pickups.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add -5.0 terminal penalty on timeout (bypasses reward clamp)

When an episode is truncated (timeout), apply a -5.0 penalty that
bypasses the per-step [-1, 1] reward clamp. This ensures GAE propagates
a strong negative signal backward through the episode, preventing the
model from learning that stalling with positive rewards is acceptable.

Uses StepRewards.terminal_penalty field added to rewards.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "Add -5.0 terminal penalty on timeout (bypasses reward clamp)"

This reverts commit 9cecea288be4e12ffee7393cc5a7d07421f318ed.

* Simplify Timeout: keep position-stuck and no-discovery only

Removed no_next_room_timeout (300 steps was too aggressive, cutting off
episodes before the room stalling penalty could do its job) and tile
revisit timeout (redundant with room step stalling penalty in critic).

Remaining timeouts:
- position_timeout=50: genuine stuck-in-wall detection
- no_discovery_timeout=2000: safety net for truly lost episodes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Scale back room stalling penalty: flat -0.01 ramping to -0.02

Previous penalty escalated quadratically and dominated all other signals
(-40 to -120 per episode vs ~10 for PBRS). Replace with gentle linear
ramp: -0.01/step after 150 grace, slowly increasing to -0.02/step over
1850 steps. Max total penalty over full 2000 steps ~= -28 vs PBRS ~10.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

tensorflow 2.21.0 no longer bundles tensorboard as a transitive
